data.narrative:
Safe Superintelligence Inc., founded by Ilya Sutskever, Daniel Gross, and Daniel Levy, is a Series B AI & Software company with a stated post-money valuation of $32.0B and total capital raised exceeding $1B. The peer universe comprises one public comparable (Palantir) and eight private peers in AI & Software, including Physical Intelligence ($5.6B), World Labs ($5.0B), Nominal ($1.0B), and Photoroom ($500M).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B to subsequent-stage advancement, implies a forward valuation of $60.8B. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median of $3.0B, with a range of $500M to $5.6B. The divergence between these methods reflects SSI's substantial capital raise and stated valuation relative to earlier-stage peers in the comparable set.

Triangulation across both methods yields a range of $2.5B (low) to $69.9B (high), with a median of $31.9B. The current $32.0B valuation aligns with the triangulated median, suggesting defensibility at the post-money figure. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the peer set given SSI's stage and capital raise profile, and the applicability of stage-progression multiples to frontier AI development rather than conventional software progression.
